decision: Drive the bell off the shared Attention status model (status==="waiting"), not a direct Watchtower notification feed.
watchtower_investigation: Verified against watchtower Go source: NO REST endpoint exposes notification events (events table is write-only; no GetSessionEvents query, no /api/.../events route). Only real-time surface is /ws — an unauthenticated, unscoped, fire-and-forget, no-replay broadcast of ALL ingested messages. It DOES relay 'notification' events with envelope session_id + payload {level,message}, but: (a) payload is thin (level+message only; no transcript path, no tool, no structured permission fields), (b) out-of-the-box NO notification hook is installed so nothing emits them, (c) no replay. Files: internal/ws/server.go:69-107,182-196; pkg/protocol/message.go:19,40-46,98-102; internal/api/api.go:34-47; internal/db/db.go:287-293.
consequence: The REST-backed Attention board (#153) cannot source 'waiting' from live data today, so the live board/bell will not surface 🟡 until Watchtower gains a readable notification source (a /ws bridge or a new /api/sessions/{id}/events REST endpoint). Bell keys on status===waiting so it lights up automatically with zero code change the moment that lands. Rejected building a durable /ws bridge through Next.js: fragile across host+container modes, unauthenticated/unscoped, no replay — same fragility #153 already documented in useAttentionPoll.ts.
live_vs_proxy: Infra (bell/badge/dedup/browser-notification/settings-toggle) fully built + tested and driven by the shared status model. The transition SIGNAL is deferred on Watchtower exposing readable notification events. Nothing faked.
resilience: Priming suppresses popup storms on load/reconnect; aggregate cap (>3) collapses mass-block into one popup; conn-gating ignores transient outages; poll continues while tab hidden so alerts fire when away; per-session OS tag de-dups at browser layer.

decision: Fold gh-153's live WebSocket feed into gh-154's SHARED single-flight attention source (lib/attention/source.ts), not into the per-hook useAttentionPoll.
context: gh-153's WS commit (9a776af) put WS-live client logic directly inside useAttentionPoll.ts. gh-154 had already refactored that hook into a thin useSyncExternalStore binding over a process-wide singleton source so the board AND the always-mounted notification bell share ONE poller. Merging gh-153 conflicted on hooks/useAttentionPoll.ts and tests/lib/watchtower-client.test.ts.
choice: Merged origin/gh-153; kept HEAD's thin delegate hook (took --ours). Moved the WS-live consumption INTO source.ts so there is exactly ONE shared socket for the whole app: on first subscribe the source opens one WS to the terminal-server ?stream=attention bridge via the existing ticket-aware openTerminalWebSocket (no new API route -> audit:auth clean); onopen resyncs the REST snapshot and switches the poll to a 30s safety-resync cadence (effectiveDelay caps to SAFETY_RESYNC_MS while wsLive); onmessage applies deltas via the pure reducer lib/attention/live.ts applyLiveEvent/parseWsMessage (same-ref bail-out skips needless renders); onclose/onerror resume the fast poll fallback and schedule an exponential-backoff reconnect (paused while hidden); closeWs on last-unsubscribe and on visibility changes reconnect+resync. conn stays REST-driven (deltas never flip conn), preserving gh-154's retain-cards-on-transient-failure semantics.
conflicts: hooks/useAttentionPoll.ts -> kept HEAD thin hook. tests/lib/watchtower-client.test.ts (add/add) -> unioned both suites (reachability/array-parse + field-coercion). Removed tests/hooks/use-attention-poll.test.tsx (gh-153's in-hook WS + clear-on-failure test) as obsolete against gh-154's thin-hook + retain-on-failure source; relocated its WS coverage (reconnect-resync, tool_post delta, session_end removal, malformed-frame guard, poll fallback) into tests/lib/attention-source.test.ts, mocking @/lib/websocket-utils so the REST-cadence cases stay isolated.
gates: lint 0 errors; typecheck clean; audit:auth 0 new offenders; vitest 1734 passed / 2 skipped (118 files); build exit 0; prettier clean on all touched files (10 pre-existing unrelated drift files left untouched).
validation: producer Claude Fable 5; separate-model validation pending per repo policy.
